rows.notes: Page title at top; fully legible, no amounts present. Clear script and unambiguous.
Second line of the heading. Words are legible though abbreviations present; no monetary columns used.
Third line of heading; date and Latin phrase readable; no amounts.
Fourth line of heading; clearly reads the completion of the heading phrase and date.
Columns located by aligning the clearly spaced totals below. Pounds (229) and shillings (13) are clear; pence shows '8' with a small trailing loop interpreted as 'ob' = halfpenny (0.5).
Pounds 163 and shillings 18 are clear. Pence column contains a dash/blank — interpreted as zero pence; column alignment confirmed by neighbouring rows.
Clear reading: 50 pounds, shillings column blank (interpreted 0), pence '4' legible and aligned with pence column.
Pounds appear as a four-digit '1151' (visible leading '1'); shillings '7' and pence '1' are clear and align to the ruled columns.

A subtotal line (no printed description) directly under the preceding receipts. Numeric groups align with the three ruled columns; the pence shows '1' with a halfpenny loop (0.5). Slight uncertainty because the label is absent; numbers themselves are clear.
Label 'Debit. Anni procedentis' is clear. Amount reads 489 | 7 | 5 with a three‑quarters mark (appears as '3q' or '3/4') interpreted as 0.75. Column alignment confirmed by surrounding totals.
Summa (total) of receipts — text and numbers are legible. Pence shows a three‑quarters mark (0.75). Values align well in the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Section heading for payments (soluta). Clear text, no amounts on this line.

Pounds read as 1084 (four digits visible). Shillings column shows no figure (taken as 0). Pence reads '8' with a three‑quarters mark (0.75). Slight uncertainty in the shilling being blank vs faint mark, but column alignment with other rows supports 0 shillings.
Clear reading: 147 | 15 | 6. Handwriting and alignment unambiguous.
Pounds read as 708 (the '7' looks slightly compressed but context and column alignment confirm it). Shillings 17 clear; pence 2 with a three‑quarters mark (0.75) is visible.
Subtotal of payments. Text 'Summa Solutorum' and the total 1940 | 13 | 5 1/2 are clearly written; halfpenny indicated by loop = 0.5.
Mirrors the earlier receipts total. Numbers align and show three‑quarters on pence (0.75); label 'Ex Receptis' readable.
'Deductis Solutis' is a deduction/total line matching the payments subtotal. Clear alignment; pence shows halfpenny (0.5). This is a calculated deduction line, so labeled as total.
Remainder ('Rest.') given as 151 | 13 | 1 3/4 (pence shows three‑quarters = 0.75). Clear and aligned; this is a resulting balance so marked as total.

rows.description: Except of Rectis Profits, see account
rows.notes: Text is abbreviated/faded but reads as exception of receipts/profits with a referral to another account. Amount appears as 48 | 9 | 4 but the pence digit is slightly faint; column alignment supports this reading. Moderate uncertainty in the exact wording of the label.
Underlined remainder to be paid: clearly 200 | 2 | 2. Underline and alignment make this very legible.
Short Latin phrase; no monetary amounts. Legible and clearly a note/header line.
Verification heading; text readable though abbreviation 'No1' is present. No amounts.
Signature/name line. Handwriting is cursive and somewhat compressed; interpreted as 'H. Richards' followed by an abbreviation (likely office/title). No amounts.
Signature/name line 'J. Cole' visible; the following office title is abbreviated and faint. No monetary data. Moderate uncertainty in exact trailing letters.
Name looks like 'J. Rodes' with 'Decanum' (dean) or similar office noted; ink is slightly faded but readable enough to record as a header.
Signature line very cursive and partly compressed; read as 'Tho Smyth...' with bursarial abbreviation. High uncertainty in exact spelling; no amounts present.
Another officer/signature line. Handwriting faint and abbreviated; interpreted conservatively. No monetary values.
Final signature line at bottom right. Very faint/cursive; read as 'Jacob Reed' with theological abbreviation. Considerable uncertainty in exact trailing letters.
